name: Sauce Labs API
what_it_does: Cloud-based test automation platform API for running Selenium, Cypress, Playwright, and Appium tests across real and virtual browsers and devices with detailed analytics.
use_cases: Orchestrating automated test runs across browser/device matrix
Querying test results and artifacts from CI/CD pipeline agents
Managing test data and insights across teams
Real device testing for mobile app automation
Error analysis and test failure triage automation
not_for: Small teams where per-minute pricing doesn't justify cloud testing
best_when: You need enterprise test automation with strong analytics, real device coverage, and deep CI/CD integration support.
avoid_when: Your tests are simple and don't require cross-browser validation, or cost is a primary concern.

auth.notes: Username and access key as HTTP Basic Auth. Separate credentials for US and EU data centers. Tokens can be generated for team access management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free trial with limited VM minutes
pricing.notes: VM-minutes based pricing. Real device cloud is additional. Enterprise includes unlimited concurrency options.

performance.rate_limits: REST API: rate limited but limits not published; session limits by plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API errors include message and status. Some legacy endpoints have inconsistent error formats.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Test session creation is not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: US and EU data centers use different API base URLs and credentials - agents must target the correct region
Sauce Labs has multiple product lines (VDC, RDC, API Testing) with different endpoint namespaces
Job assets (screenshots, videos, logs) are deleted after 30 days - download before expiry
Tunnel (SauceConnect) for localhost testing requires running a separate daemon process
Test result status must be explicitly set via API call at test end - auto-detection is unreliable

security_score_components.security_notes: Browser/mobile testing platform. Username + access key auth. Test data in sessions may contain PII — check retention. Separate accounts per project for isolation.
